message:
Fix OnceCell set to retry on cancelled initializer and consolidate Pool waiter position tracking

Two sync primitive improvements:

1. OnceCell::set() — handle cancelled concurrent initializer (once_cell.rs)

   Previously, when set() encountered the INITIALIZING state (another
   thread/task was mid-initialization), it immediately returned Err,
   treating the cell as permanently claimed. This was incorrect when the
   other initializer could be cancelled (e.g. async task dropped),
   leaving the cell in a state where it would transition back to UNINIT
   but all concurrent set() callers had already given up.

   Now set() calls wait_for_init_blocking() when it sees INITIALIZING,
   then checks is_initialized(). If the cell was successfully
   initialized, returns Err (value already set, correct behavior). If
   the initializer was cancelled and state is back to UNINIT, loops to
   retry the CAS. This ensures set() only fails when the cell truly
   contains a value.

2. Pool WaitForPermit::poll — unified position tracking (pool.rs)

   Refactors the waiter registration and position lookup into a single
   pass. Previously, position was computed in a separate closure that
   could return inconsistent results (e.g. returning Some(len) for a
   not-yet-registered waiter). Now the position index is computed
   inline as a direct result of each registration path:
   - Existing waiter found: returns its current index
   - Re-registration at front (after failed health check): returns 0
   - New registration at back: returns the pre-push length
   The unified position is then compared against available capacity in
   a single check, eliminating the disconnected two-phase lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
